words.definition: to leave someone or something completely and not return

words.example: The bus came to an abrupt halt at the crossing.

words.example: The idea of a swimming elephant sounded absurd — until they saw one.

words.definition: to go somewhere with someone
words.example: A teacher must accompany pupils on the trip.

words.definition: to succeed in doing something
words.example: They accomplished the climb in record time.

words.definition: to gather or build up over time
words.example: Dust had accumulated on every shelf of the attic.

words.definition: correct in all details; exact
words.example: Her accurate throw hit the target dead centre.

words.definition: to come to own or learn something
words.example: He acquired a taste for olives on holiday.

words.definition: to change in order to suit new conditions
words.example: Desert plants adapt cleverly to the lack of rain.

words.definition: to regard with respect or warm approval
words.example: The crowd admired the skill of the young gymnast.

words.definition: to decorate or add beauty to
words.example: Fairy lights adorned every stall at the winter fair.

words.definition: a gentle feeling of fondness or liking
words.example: The old dog showed its affection by leaning against her.

words.definition: extreme physical or mental suffering
words.example: He was in agony after twisting his ankle.

words.definition: quick to notice and respond to danger or change
words.example: The alert guide spotted the snake before anyone stepped near.

words.definition: to change or make different
words.example: One phone call altered all their plans.

words.definition: a strong desire to achieve something
words.example: Her ambition is to become a marine biologist.

words.definition: to walk at a slow, relaxed pace
words.example: They ambled along the riverbank, feeding the ducks.

words.definition: friendly and pleasant
words.example: Our amiable neighbour always waves from his garden.

words.definition: belonging to the very distant past
words.example: The ancient castle has stood for nine hundred years.

words.definition: to expect or look forward to something
words.example: We anticipate a big crowd for sports day.

words.definition: to make a serious request; to be attractive
words.example: The head teacher appealed for volunteers to tidy the hall.

words.definition: to show approval by clapping
words.example: The audience applauded until their hands ached.

words.definition: suitable or proper for the situation
words.example: Wellies are appropriate footwear for a muddy walk.

words.definition: close to the actual, but not completely exact
words.example: Give an approximate answer to the nearest ten.

words.definition: behaving as if you are better than others
words.example: The arrogant knight refused all advice before the joust.

words.definition: to gather together; to fit parts together
words.example: The whole school assembled in the playground for the drill.

words.definition: to help someone
words.example: Two prefects assisted the librarian with the book sale.

words.definition: to surprise or impress greatly
words.example: The card trick astonished everyone at the party.

words.definition: paying close attention; considerate
words.example: The attentive waiter refilled every glass before being asked.

words.definition: able to be heard
words.example: Her whisper was barely audible at the back of the room.

words.definition: to turn away; to prevent something bad
words.example: Quick thinking averted a nasty accident at the crossing.

words.definition: a feeling of great respect mixed with wonder
words.example: The children stared in awe at the blue whale skeleton.

words.definition: unable to produce plants or fruit; empty
words.example: Nothing grew on the barren, windswept plain.

words.definition: shy and easily embarrassed
words.example: The bashful new boy hid behind his mother at the gate.

words.definition: to signal someone to come closer with a gesture
words.example: The guide beckoned the group towards the hidden doorway.

words.definition: to shout in a deep, loud voice
words.example: The sergeant bellowed orders across the field.

words.definition: to be disloyal to someone who trusts you; to reveal unintentionally
words.example: He would never betray a friend's secret.

words.definition: bare and cold; offering little hope
words.example: The moor looked bleak under the grey January sky.

words.definition: a careless or embarrassing mistake
words.example: Forgetting the tickets was an expensive blunder.

words.definition: to talk with too much pride about oneself
words.example: He boasted about his high score until everyone groaned.

words.definition: a generous amount; a reward offered
words.example: The orchard produced a bounty of apples that autumn.

words.definition: quick and energetic; pleasantly cold
words.example: A brisk walk to school warmed them up.

words.definition: a heavy load; a cause of worry or hardship
words.example: Carrying the water was a daily burden for the villagers.

words.definition: an event causing great damage or distress
words.example: Losing the harvest to floods was a calamity for the farm.

words.definition: a person or animal that has been caught and held
words.example: The captives were released when the ransom was paid.

words.definition: relaxed and unconcerned; not regular or formal
words.example: He gave a casual wave as if nothing had happened.

words.definition: complete disorder and confusion
words.example: The classroom descended into chaos when the hamster escaped.

words.definition: to protect and care for lovingly; to treasure
words.example: She cherished the locket her grandmother gave her.

words.definition: to communicate a message; to transport
words.example: The painting conveys a feeling of deep peace.

words.definition: polite and courteous; relating to citizens
words.example: Despite their argument, they stayed civil at dinner.

words.definition: the quality of being clear and easy to understand
words.example: She explained the rules with perfect clarity.

words.definition: a continuous rattling sound of hard objects
words.example: The clatter of pans announced breakfast downstairs.

words.definition: to gently persuade someone to do something
words.example: She coaxed the frightened cat down from the tree.

words.definition: to begin
words.example: The ceremony will commence at ten o'clock sharp.

words.definition: a state of confused and noisy disturbance
words.example: A commotion in the corridor interrupted the lesson.

words.definition: a person or animal one spends time with
words.example: The old dog had been her constant companion for years.

words.definition: to force someone to do something
words.example: Bad weather compelled the climbers to turn back.

words.definition: having the ability to do something well enough
words.example: A competent swimmer can cross the bay easily.

words.definition: to create music or writing; to calm oneself
words.example: She composed a song for the school concert.

words.definition: feeling sure of oneself or of something
words.example: He walked into the interview feeling calm and confident.

words.definition: to overcome and take control of; to defeat
words.example: She finally conquered her fear of heights on the zip wire.

words.definition: permission or agreement for something to happen
words.example: You need a parent's consent to join the trip.

words.definition: to give something in order to help achieve a shared aim
words.example: Everyone contributed a cake to the charity sale.

words.definition: to recognise the difference between things
words.example: It is hard to distinguish the twins at first glance.

words.definition: to crouch down in fear
words.example: The puppy cowered under the table during the thunder.

words.definition: to want something very strongly
words.example: After the long hike he craved a hot dinner.

words.definition: sad and disappointed
words.example: She looked crestfallen when the fair was cancelled.

words.definition: to shrink back in fear or embarrassment
words.example: He cringed as his dad danced at the wedding.

words.definition: extremely important; decisive
words.example: The last question was crucial to winning the quiz.

words.definition: roughly made; lacking polish or taste
words.example: They built a crude raft from logs and rope.

words.definition: to grow crops; to develop a skill or quality
words.example: The monks cultivated herbs in the walled garden.

words.definition: a strong desire to know or learn something
words.example: Curiosity drew the children towards the old chest.

words.definition: a flow of water or air; the present time (adj)
words.example: The strong current carried the boat downstream.

words.definition: small, delicate, and pretty
words.example: The doll wore dainty silver shoes.

words.definition: willing to take bold risks
words.example: The daring rescue made the front page.

words.definition: to blind briefly with bright light; to amaze
words.example: The skater dazzled the judges with her final jump.

words.definition: scattered pieces of rubbish or wreckage
words.example: The storm left debris all over the playground.

words.definition: a period of ten years
words.example: The bridge took nearly a decade to build.

words.definition: of an acceptable standard; kind and honest
words.example: He's a decent goalkeeper and a fair captain.

words.definition: to devote time or effort to a purpose
words.example: She dedicated every Saturday to violin practice.

words.definition: easily broken or damaged; finely made
words.example: The delicate butterfly wings shimmered in the sun.

words.definition: to show clearly how something works or is true
words.example: The coach demonstrated the perfect serve.

words.definition: closely packed together; thick
words.example: They lost sight of each other in the dense fog.

words.definition: to leave, especially to start a journey
words.example: The ferry departs from the harbour at noon.

words.definition: trustworthy and reliable
words.example: A dependable friend always keeps their promise.

words.definition: feeling hopeless and ready to take any risk
words.example: The stranded sailors grew desperate for fresh water.

words.definition: the place to which someone is travelling
words.example: After two flights and a bus, they reached their destination.

words.definition: to discover or notice something hidden
words.example: The dog detected the scent long before we saw anything.

words.definition: firmly decided; refusing to give up
words.example: She was determined to finish the race, blisters and all.

words.definition: to dislike intensely
words.example: He detests the smell of boiled cabbage.

words.definition: to give all or most of one's time or resources to something
words.example: She devotes her weekends to the animal shelter.

words.definition: a feeling of unhappiness and disappointment
words.example: To their dismay, the ice-cream van drove straight past.

words.definition: extreme worry, sadness, or pain
words.example: The lifeboat answered a call from a yacht in distress.

words.definition: extreme in effect; severe
words.example: Cutting the whole tree down seemed a drastic solution.

words.definition: to give something, especially to a charity
words.example: They donated their old books to the library sale.

words.definition: great fear about something that may happen
words.example: He felt a growing dread as the dentist called his name.

words.definition: to wet something thoroughly
words.example: A sudden downpour drenched the entire team.

words.definition: sleepy and slow to react
words.example: The warm classroom made everyone drowsy after lunch.

words.definition: able to last a long time without breaking
words.example: School shoes need to be durable as well as comfortable.

words.definition: to live in a place; to think about at length
words.example: Owls dwell in the hollow trunks of old trees.

words.definition: to become gradually smaller or weaker
words.example: Their food supplies dwindled as the snow kept falling.

words.definition: full of keen desire or enthusiasm
words.example: The eager puppies tumbled towards their food bowls.

words.definition: serious and sincere in intention
words.example: She made an earnest promise to practise every day.

words.definition: safe and suitable to eat
words.example: Only pick mushrooms you are certain are edible.

words.definition: graceful and stylish
words.example: The swan glided across the lake in one elegant movement.

words.definition: to raise to a higher position or level
words.example: The nurse said to elevate the sprained ankle on a cushion.

words.definition: to come out into view; to become known
words.example: A badger emerged from the hedge at dusk.

words.definition: to meet unexpectedly; to face something difficult
words.example: The hikers encountered a herd of deer on the ridge.

words.definition: very unusual or remarkable
words.example: The magician performed an extraordinary escape underwater.

words.definition: to completely surround or cover
words.example: Flames engulfed the old barn within minutes.

words.definition: to make someone very angry
words.example: The unfair penalty enraged the home supporters.

words.definition: to improve the quality or value of something
words.example: Reading widely enriches your vocabulary.

words.definition: to give someone responsibility for something valuable
words.example: She entrusted her diary to her best friend.

words.definition: to wrap up or surround completely
words.example: Thick mist enveloped the valley by evening.

words.definition: to supply with what is needed
words.example: The expedition was equipped with ropes and radios.

words.definition: to accompany someone for protection or as a courtesy
words.example: A prefect escorted the visitors to the hall.

words.definition: to set up on a firm basis; to prove
words.example: The club was established over a century ago.

words.definition: respect and admiration
words.example: The retiring teacher was held in great esteem.

words.definition: lasting forever
words.example: The two friends swore eternal loyalty.

words.definition: to go beyond a limit or expectation
words.example: Ticket sales exceeded all expectations.

words.definition: to cry out suddenly with strong emotion
words.example: 'We won!' she exclaimed, leaping off the bench.

words.definition: to become or make larger
words.example: Metal expands when it is heated.

words.definition: a journey undertaken for a particular purpose
words.example: The expedition set out to map the frozen coastline.

words.definition: extreme tiredness
words.example: Fatigue finally forced the runners to rest.

words.definition: lacking physical strength; weak
words.example: His feeble excuse convinced no one.

words.definition: producing abundant crops or growth
words.example: The fertile valley fed three villages.

words.definition: violently aggressive; intense
words.example: The mother swan is fierce in defence of her cygnets.

words.definition: to run away from danger
words.example: The rabbits fled at the sound of footsteps.

words.definition: weak and easily damaged; unconvincing
words.example: The flimsy umbrella turned inside out in the wind.

words.definition: an enemy or opponent
words.example: The old rivals shook hands, friends now rather than foes.

words.definition: to be aware of something before it happens
words.example: Nobody could foresee how popular the club would become.

words.definition: having a pleasant, sweet smell
words.example: The kitchen was fragrant with baking bread.

words.definition: wild with fear, anxiety, or haste
words.example: There was a frantic search for the missing keys.

words.definition: to worry constantly about something
words.example: Don't fret about the test — you've prepared well.

words.definition: brave and heroic; courteous
words.example: The gallant knight offered the traveller his own horse.

words.definition: to shine brightly with reflected light
words.example: The polished trophies gleamed in the cabinet.

words.definition: to move smoothly and effortlessly
words.example: The skaters glided across the frozen pond.

words.definition: a brief or partial view
words.example: They caught a glimpse of the deer before it vanished.

words.definition: very serious or solemn
words.example: The doctor's grave expression told them the news was bad.
